intro:
Pilot skills are probably the second most common thing that I often see newer/returning players mess up (the first being specializations) so with this post, I hope to present a concise guide that makes picking the best pilot skills for your robots and titans a breeze.
Broadly, we can group pilot skills into four categories:
Must use
Usually use
Sometimes use
Don't use (unless you know what you're doing)
Pilot skill priority is different for robots and titans, since even some of their skills that share the same name and general function don't all quite work the same, so lets go over each individually.

Must Use.name: Mechanic
Must Use.description: Playing the game without this skill feels awful. Some bots with extremely strong healing can get away without using it, but for the majority of bots, this should be an easy pick

Must Use.name: Armor Expert
Must Use.description: An easy pick for some free durability

Must Use.name: Road Hog
Must Use.description: An easy pick for some free speed

Must Use.name: Deft/Clever Survivor
Must Use.description: Almost every robot that has this skill wants it. There are a couple exceptions, so you need to use common sense: Will the ability save your ass if someone tries to kill you while it's on cooldown? If so, pick deft.

Must Use.name: True Ace
Must Use.description: Every bot that can use this should use this.

Must Use.name: Suppressor
Must Use.description: The value of this skill depends on your suppression power. Most bots that suppress do 50% or 75%, so they should definitely take this skill, but if a bot with 90% suppression comes out, it's overkill.

Must Use.name: Dodger
Must Use.description: Increased uptime on overpowered modules like UC and phase shift makes for a great skill. Can potentially justify skipping this, but only if you are extremely powercell broke.

Must Use.name: Engine Expert, Defense Expert, Ferocious Guardian
Must Use.description: You can only take one of these skills, and you absolutely should on most bots. On some bots, you'll need to use common sense to decide if it makes any sense (for example, do you really want an extra 20 defense points when Leech uses its ability to give itself 900?), but often, there is no single best choice and you can use whatever you want. Leech and Revenant currently have a multiplicative stacking interaction with their pilots (Thomas and Ricardo) which makes them EXTREMELY fast with Engine expert, way more than 10%. I believe EE is a must pick for those bots.

Usually Use.name: Stubborn Warrior
Usually Use.description: Unlike stubborn speedster, this is pretty much just guaranteed free durability since you will almost certainly get to 30% health before dying (unless you get oneshot) so you can treat it as some more free durability.

Usually Use.name: Energy Shield Expert/Crazy Electrician
Usually Use.description: For bots relying on Aegis shields, these skills can be great, but since robot pilots only have 7 slots, it can sometimes be hard to fit them both (or even one of them) in, especially if using weapons that want a weapon skill.

Usually Use.name: Modules Expert
Usually Use.description: This skill doesn’t only extend the duration of phase shift, shieldbreaker, quantum radar, or any other active module with a duration, but it also give more healing with healing modules. I usually prefer dodger though.

Usually Use.name: Sharpshooter
Usually Use.description: Most weapons with this skill available will want to take it if possible. Some weapons gain more from it than others. Ask yourself the question: Do my weapons feel like they miss a lot at the range I usually play this bot at? If yes, take sharpshooter.

Usually Use.name: Powerman
Usually Use.description: Always use this if it's available, less time spent reloading is more time spent killing!

Usually Use.name: Wonderworker
Usually Use.description: Usually best used on bots that spam their ability a lot or use their ability to try and save their own ass.

Usually Use.name: Tough Guy and Customization Adept
Usually Use.description: Easy picks on tanks, but not as useful for non tanks.

Usually Use.name: Cautious Pilot
Usually Use.description: I personally avoid this skill because I don't like being slow, but it is still justifiable on tanks.

Usually Use.name: Spy and Scout
Usually Use.description: Easy picks on Beacon cappers and Saboteurs, and can even be used on Damage Dealers and Raiders.

Usually Use.name: Destroyer
Usually Use.description: If your robot has a built in weapon that deals a significant amount of damage, you want this skill.

Usually Use.name: Physical Shield Expert
Usually Use.description: If your robot strongly relies on a physical shield that isn't unbreakable already then you want this skill.

Usually Use.name: Foolhardy Quartermaster
Usually Use.description: Similar to deft survivor, can save you in a pinch, giving you back your module at half health.

Sometimes Use.name: Adamant Road Hog
Sometimes Use.description: This is the only “Adamant” skill that is worth using if you have space available. It gives a relevant boost (speed while you don’t have beacons) and the multiplier is higher than any other adamant skill.

Sometimes Use.name: Invulnerable Raider
Sometimes Use.description: This is the only “Raider” skill that is worth using if you have space available. It can be used on opener bots to have some defense points at the start of the match.

Sometimes Use.name: Master Gunsmith
Sometimes Use.description: 5% damage for free isn’t useless or anything, but you usually don’t have enough space to take an unimpactful skill like this except on some saboteurs who are lacking in damage since they dont have NA.

Sometimes Use.name: Survivor and Thrill Seeker
Sometimes Use.description: Most bots that want damage don't have room to take these skills, just like with master gunsmith.

Sometimes Use.name: Guidance Operator
Sometimes Use.description: The problem with this skill is that all the weapons it applies to suck, except boom family, which are mid but you should use this for them.

Don't Use.name: Adamant Mechanic, Adamant Gunsmith
Don't Use.description: Unless you have a second hangar identical to your first that you only spawn in while you're losing, these skills are pretty hard to justify using.

Don't Use.name: Raider Mechanic, Ninja
Don't Use.description: These skills are hard to justify slotting in.

Don't Use.name: "Opportunist" skills, Bounty hunter
Don't Use.description: These skills are OP in PvE but useless in normal modes.

Don't Use.name: Gunsmith (Weapon Specific)
Don't Use.description: Hard to justify taking a 4.5% damage boost that doesn’t apply to all weapons (unless you’re using 4 of the same exact weapon, but even then, you’ll run out of slots before you get to this point).

Don't Use.name: Speed Shooter
Don't Use.description: Usually not worth the slot

Don't Use.name: Stubborn Speedster
Don't Use.description: Not entirely useless but generally worse than stubborn warrior

Don't Use.name: Quartermaster
Don't Use.description: This skill doesn't give buffs, but if you're super powercell broke I guess you can use it?

Don't Use.name: Traditionalist, Daredevil, Ghost
Don't Use.description: Technically these skills are not completely unusable, with Falcon and Fafnir making for acceptable tanks via Traditionalist, and Ghost being okay on some older/legacy bots that can use it, but 95% of the time you're throwing if you pick this or use a bot where its optimal to pick this.

Don't Use.name: Explosives Expert
Don't Use.description: You will not notice the increase in splash size from this skill

Don't Use.name: True Cowboy
Don't Use.description: The problem with this skill is that all the robots it applies to suck

Don't Use.name: Knight Errant
Don't Use.description: Ecu sucks

Must Use.name: Mechanic
Must Use.description: Playing the game without this skill feels awful. Titans like this skill especially.

Must Use.name: Road Hog
Must Use.description: An easy pick for some free speed

Must Use.name: Deft/Clever Survivor
Must Use.description: Almost every titan that has this skill wants it. There are a couple exceptions, so you need to use common sense: Will the ability save your ass if someone tries to kill you while it's on cooldown? If so, pick deft.

Must Use.name: True Ace
Must Use.description: Every titan that can use this should use this.

Must Use.name: Defense Expert, Ferocious Guardian
Must Use.description: Titans should usually take both of these, depending on the ability it affects. Obviously don't take ferocious guardian on an ability that disables your weapons, lol.

Must Use.name: Titan Master
Must Use.description: Easy pick to get your titan out quicker.

Must Use.name: Titan Slayer
Must Use.description: Easy pick for free damage against titans.

Must Use.name: Armor Expert
Must Use.description: Only gives 6% durability, worth using but less of a priority than it is on robots.

Must Use.name: Stubborn Warrior
Must Use.description: Unlike stubborn speedster, this is pretty much just guaranteed free durability since you will almost certainly get to 30% health before dying (unless you get oneshot) so you can treat it as some more free durability. Especially powerful while TRA is active.

Usually Use.name: Jaeger
Usually Use.description: For titans with 1 Alpha and 2 Beta slots, Jaeger is comparable to taking another copy of titan slayer, which is obviously good.

Usually Use.name: Sharpshooter
Usually Use.description: Most weapons with this skill available will want to take it if possible. Some weapons gain more from it than others. Ask yourself the question: Do my weapons feel like they miss a lot at the range I usually play this bot at? If yes, take sharpshooter.

Usually Use.name: Suppressor
Usually Use.description: The value of this skill depends on your suppression power. Most titans that suppress do 50% or 75%, so they should definitely take this skill, but for UE Nodens who has a 90% suppression, this skill is overkill.

Usually Use.name: Wonderworker
Usually Use.description: Usually best used on titans that spam their ability a lot or use their ability to try and save their own ass.

Usually Use.name: Cautious Pilot and Customization Adept
Usually Use.description: Easy picks on tanks.

Usually Use.name: Survivor and Thrill Seeker
Usually Use.description: Somewhat justifiable on damage dealers that don't really need speed or durability to do their job (ie Murometz).

Usually Use.name: Destroyer
Usually Use.description: If your titan has a built in weapon that deals a significant amount of damage, you want this skill.

Usually Use.name: Physical Shield Expert
Usually Use.description: If your titan strongly relies on a physical shield then you want this skill.

Usually Use.name: Master Gunsmith
Usually Use.description: If you don't want to deal with the condition of reduced durability or speed, this is like Survivor or Thrill Seeker with half effectiveness and no downside.

Sometimes Use.name: Tough Guy
Sometimes Use.description: Only take this skill on Mauler

Sometimes Use.name: Bounty Hunter
Sometimes Use.description: Gives a decent damage boost but the uptime is short. Viable on some Quantum Sensor titans since they are missing the damage from TNA. Broken in PvE.

Sometimes Use.name: Wise Opportunist
Sometimes Use.description: Gives a big defense boost that can be useful for TRA titans that are under fire by many enemies at once, such as Ultimate Ming.

Don't Use.name: Engine Expert
Don't Use.description: Can't be taken alongside road hog, and it's strictly worse than road hog

Don't Use.name: Cunning Opportunist
Don't Use.description: Some speed on kills isn’t really justifiable.

Don't Use.name: Spy and Scout
Don't Use.description: These kind of suck for titans, since they don't want to trade off their durability or damage for speed

Don't Use.name: "Adamant" skills
Don't Use.description: On titans, these skills don't even give enough stats to be worth picking at all.

Don't Use.name: "Raider" skills, Ninja
Don't Use.description: On titans, these skills don't even give enough stats to be worth picking at all.

Don't Use.name: Speed Shooter
Don't Use.description: Usually not worth the slot

Don't Use.name: Stubborn Speedster
Don't Use.description: Not entirely useless but generally worse than stubborn warrior

Don't Use.name: Traditionalist, Daredevil, Ghost
Don't Use.description: Just... don't.

Don't Use.name: Explosives Expert
Don't Use.description: You will not notice the increase in splash size from this skill

Don't Use.name: Guidance Operator
Don't Use.description: The problem with this skill is that all the weapons it applies to suck.

Don't Use.name: Generalist, Heavyweight Battler, Rationalist, Hothead
Don't Use.description: Their boosts are not enough to justify taking most of the time.

Don't Use.name: Berserker
Don't Use.description: Multiply your titan's HP by 0.05 to understand how rare it will be to actually have this skill activate for any meaningful amount of time.

Don't Use.name: Hoplite
Don't Use.description: You have probably never heard of this skill before. That is for a good reason. Very good for players who like when their titan instantly wilts after someone with venires looks at them. That should be all... let me know if I missed anything or you have any further pilot related questions!
